Go to your [`Firebase Console`](https://console.firebase.google.com/) | MCP:uXl1oM | | MCP:uXl1oM | 2. Click on `Add project`. | MCP:uXl1oM | | MCP:uXl1oM | - Follow steps and create project. | MCP:uXl1oM | | MCP:uXl1oM | 3. Click on web icon. | MCP:uXl1oM | | MCP:uXl1oM | - Register web app. | MCP:uXl1oM | - You will get details for setting up `Firebase SDK`. | MCP:uXl1oM | | MCP:uXl1oM | 4. Add the config details in your `.env` file (available in root of your project). | MCP:uXl1oM | | MCP:uXl1oM | ### SetUp SignIn with google | MCP:uXl1oM | | MCP:uXl1oM | 1. In your project console, click on `Authentication` and `Get Started`. | MCP:uXl1oM | | MCP:uXl1oM | 2. In `Sign-in method`, click on `Google`. | MCP:uXl1oM | | MCP:uXl1oM | 3. data.content:
I now have all the key information. Here's a complete summary:

---

## How to Install, Configure, and Deploy This React Firebase Starter

### 1. Installation

Create a new React app using this CRA (Create React App) template:

```bash
npx create-react-app your-project-name --template firebase-starter
```

Or with Yarn:

```bash
yarn create react-app your-project-name --template firebase-starter
```

This will scaffold a project pre-configured with:
- **React 18** + **react-router-dom v6** (routing with private/protected route support)
- **Firebase v9** (with Google Authentication via `GoogleAuthProvider`)
- **Material UI (MUI v5)** (login screen UI)
- **gh-pages** (deployment to GitHub Pages)
- **SPA GitHub Pages** routing support (via a `public/404.html` redirect trick)

---

### 2. Configure Firebase

**a. Create a Firebase project:**
1. Go to the [Firebase Console](https://console.firebase.google.com/) and click **Add project**.
2. Follow the wizard to create the project.
3. Click the **Web** icon to register a web app — Firebase will give you SDK configuration values.

**b. Set environment variables:**

In your project root, edit the `.env` file (the template provides one with placeholder values). Replace the placeholders with your real Firebase config:

```
REACT_APP_FirebaseApiKey=<your-api-key>
REACT_APP_FirebaseAuthDomain=<your-auth-domain>
REACT_APP_FirebaseProjectId=<your-project-id>
REACT_APP_FirebaseStorageBucket=<your-storage-bucket>
REACT_APP_FirebaseMessagingSenderId=<your-sender-id>
REACT_APP_FirebaseAppId=<your-app-id>
```

These are read by `src/config/firebase.config.js` at build time.

**c. Enable Google Sign-In:**
1. In the Firebase Console, go to **Authentication → Get Started**.
2. Under **Sign-in method**, click **Google**, toggle **Enable**, and save.

---

### 3. Deploy to GitHub Pages

**a. Add your GitHub Pages domain to Firebase's authorized domains:**
- In the Firebase Console → **Authentication → Settings → Authorized domains**, add `<your-github-username>.github.io`.

**b. Set the `homepage` field in `package.json`:**

Update it to your actual GitHub Pages URL:

```json
"homepage": "http://<your-github-username>.github.io/<your-repo-name>"
```

**c. (Optional) Test the production build locally:**

```bash
npm install -g serve
npm run build-and-serve
```

(This temporarily sets `homepage` to `/` for local testing.)

**d. Deploy:**

```bash
npm run deploy
```

This runs the `deploy` script defined in `package.json`:
- `predeploy` → `npm run build` (creates a production build in `build/`)
- `deploy` → `gh-pages -d build` (pushes the `build/` folder to the `gh-pages` branch)

GitHub Pages will serve the app from the `gh-pages` branch. The repo includes a `public/404.html` that implements the [SPA GitHub Pages redirect hack](https://github.com/rafgraph/spa-github-pages), so client-side routing works correctly (deep links redirect to `index.html` rather than returning a real 404).

---

### Quick Reference – Available Scripts

| Script | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `npm start` | Run dev server locally |
| `npm run build` | Create production build |
| `npm run deploy` | Build + deploy to GitHub Pages |
| `npm test` | Run tests |

### Adding New Screens

1. Create a component in `src/screens/`.
2. Add a route entry in `src/config/routes.js`:

```js
{
  path: "/your-route",
  component: YourScreenComponentName,
  name: "Screen Name",
  protected: false, // set to true if authentication is required
}
```
